108752.explanation: Residents must be able to participate in discussions and decisions about important issues. A referendum, like other ways residents can express their opinions, gives residents more influence and can increase trust in local democracy. It complements the work of the city council.

108756.explanation: People fleeing war and violence have the right to safe shelter. Asylum reception is a joint task of all municipalities in the Netherlands. The municipality must ensure good reception in our city and safety for everyone.

108761.explanation: A large fireworks show is not good for the environment, animals, and air quality. An alternative, such as a drone show, can provide a festive feeling without the drawbacks. This keeps New Year's safe and enjoyable for everyone.

108764.explanation: The municipality can help entrepreneurs make their buildings more accessible, especially if they find it difficult or cannot afford it. The municipality can also provide advice. This way, everyone can easily participate in shops, cafés, and other places.

108769.explanation: Sports clubs are important for the city. They bring people together and encourage physical activity. Money is not always necessary. The municipality can help by providing advice and sharing knowledge to keep clubs running well.

108770.explanation: Almere is only 50 years old but its history is important. Monuments make the city recognizable and tell our story. That is why we do not want to cut back. We want to invest in these buildings to preserve them for the future.

108771.explanation: We want a pleasant and safe city where people can live well and meet each other. More enforcement officers help with this: they supervise, detect problems early, and ensure the city remains safe for everyone.

108772.explanation: In Almere, camera surveillance may not be placed everywhere. It is only useful in unsafe situations or to prevent new problems. This way, the city stays safe without residents feeling constantly watched.

108774.explanation: Enforcement officers must be able to work safely. With a baton, you can use violence, which enforcement officers may not do-only the police can. Support from the police and good equipment, such as bodycams, are important. This allows enforcement officers to supervise and keep the city safe for everyone.

108775.explanation: People buying a house for the first time should also be able to do so. For professions like teachers, extra support from the municipality can help. The most important thing is that more affordable homes become available. This way, everyone in Almere can find a nice home.

108778.explanation: There is a great shortage of housing. Therefore, we do not want houses to remain empty or be bought solely to make money without living in them. Moreover, it is better for the neighborhood if homes are occupied.

108779.explanation: Many offices are vacant, and there is a great shortage of housing. Converting vacant offices into homes makes neighborhoods livelier and provides more affordable housing.

108781.explanation: Not everyone receiving social assistance can do meaningful (volunteer) work. The municipality can help those who can, to find (volunteer) work, education, or other daily activities. This allows people to actively participate in society, making them happier.

108782.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes helps people with low incomes. This way they have more money left. We prevent financial problems and ensure that everyone can participate in society.

108783.explanation: We want to make it easier to help people in poverty, for example by raising the income threshold for assistance so that no one falls through the cracks. This way, help is also available when your income is just a little higher.

108785.explanation: The basic income is a fixed amount of money that everyone receives from the government to live on. A trial with a basic income reduces people's financial worries and gives them more peace of mind. This creates more space for work, care, or education. Through this trial, the municipality learns what best helps to combat poverty.

108789.explanation: Customized transport helps residents travel independently. This is important to be able to get around the city without private transport. In new neighborhoods, shops and healthcare must be accessible by bus.

108790.explanation: We want clean and healthy air, also in the city center. Therefore, polluting vehicles are no longer allowed in the city center. This reduces air pollution and keeps the center a pleasant place to live and visit. The municipality is already working on arranging this.

108791.explanation: Parking rules will remain the same. Changes will only be made if residents themselves request them. This can happen, for example, if there are too few parking spaces or a lot of nuisance in the neighborhood.

108792.explanation: Everyone must receive good care that fits their culture or religion. People may choose this themselves, but the municipality closely monitors quality and costs. This way, good care remains affordable and available for all residents of Almere.

108793.explanation: The municipality should not only consider the price of youth care. Quality and care that is available quickly are also very important. Children and young people have the right to good care without having to wait a long time.

108794.explanation: Free tampons and sanitary pads ensure that no one has to choose between, for example, food or hygiene. This helps reduce poverty. As a result, everyone can participate in society in a healthy way. Organizations such as the food bank can help distribute these.

108797.explanation: The tourist tax has already been increased recently and does not need to be raised again in 2026. This money is used to make Almere more attractive for tourists. This way, Almere remains a pleasant place for residents and visitors.

108800.explanation: Banning solar panels on agricultural land is unnecessary. Solar panels help generate clean energy and can be well combined with the use of land for agriculture.

108802.explanation: The construction of an outdoor swimming pool has been discussed for 15 years. It is time to start building the outdoor pool at the location near the indoor pool in Muziekwijk. The municipality must ensure that the neighborhood does not suffer from visitor parking problems.

108810.explanation: A shelter for addicts is important because these people also have the right to good care and a comfortable place to live. Oosterwold is a suitable location for this. We will work together with the people of Oosterwold and the care organization to determine what is needed to ensure everyone can live comfortably and safely.

108812.explanation: Almere Pampus can only be built if there is a road over the IJmeer for public transport and cyclists. There is no support from Amsterdam and North Holland for a motorway.

108813.explanation: Almere will not organize large events like the Floriade for the time being. However, there remains room for plans and events that keep the city lively and enjoyable for residents.

108752.explanation: The VVD believes that important decisions belong with the elected municipal council, which represents residents and is accountable. Referenda often do not do justice to complex issues and can put decision-making and stability under pressure.

108756.explanation: Closing without an alternative causes more problems and uncertainty. As a municipality, we should not do that lightly. Nationally, we can do more. The VVD is against expanding asylum reception and therefore advocates strong national measures to significantly limit asylum inflow.

108761.explanation: The municipality can contribute to a festive and safe New Year's Eve with a fireworks show. That is part of our tradition. The VVD is positive about this, provided it is well organized and contributes to safety and solidarity.

108764.explanation: It is not a core task of the municipality to financially support entrepreneurs in accessibility. The VVD believes entrepreneurs are primarily responsible for this themselves.

108769.explanation: The VVD expects sports associations to take responsibility and operate financially healthy. If sports clubs are struggling, this requires a careful assessment each time, without it being mandatory.

108770.explanation: Monuments contribute to identity, tourism, and the economy. Proper maintenance prevents higher costs later. However, when it comes down to it, we find it more important to invest in safety or not to raise taxes.

108771.explanation: The VVD considers safety a top priority. By investing heavily in your safety, we can appoint extra public order officers to counter increased youth crime, nuisance in centers like Spoorbaanpad, street intimidation, and the rise in explosions.

108772.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent nuisance, stop robberies of shopkeepers, and identify offenders. The VVD therefore supports more camera surveillance.

108774.explanation: According to the VVD, public order officers must have sufficient resources to ensure your safety and their own. The desire for a baton has existed for a long time. The VVD supports this provided proper training in its use is offered.

108775.explanation: The VVD supports the starter loan so that young people of Almere truly have the chance to buy their own home in our own city.

108778.explanation: If you buy a house, it is yours. An owner-occupancy obligation restricts ownership rights. Houses can also be rented out. The VVD does not choose bans but opts for building more as a solution to the housing shortage.

108779.explanation: Converting offices into homes helps reduce the housing shortage. The VVD supports this. We must build, build, build.

108781.explanation: The VVD wants people receiving social assistance benefits to give something back, such as community service. This has proven effective because people then feel more valued, it boosts self-esteem, and helps in finding work.

108782.explanation: The VVD is against automatic tax forgiveness. Everyone benefits from the municipality's services, and we find it reasonable that every resident contributes to these services.

108783.explanation: Help must reach those who really need it. Increasing limits only increases dependency and makes the system unaffordable.

108785.explanation: A basic income costs the municipality a lot of money. Taxes would have to be raised significantly for that. The VVD does not choose that. Instead, we choose policies that create more jobs. That ultimately helps people the most.

108789.explanation: Almere has a good public transport network with bus lanes. Extra customized transport is not needed.

108790.explanation: In Almere, roads and the city center are mostly separated. A zero-emission zone solves little here. Many small entrepreneurs are also not yet ready for it. According to the VVD, sustainability should be achieved together with entrepreneurs, not through extra obligations.

108791.explanation: Extra parking permits do not solve a real problem and mainly cause higher costs. The VVD wants sufficient freedom and space for residents to live comfortably. For many people, a car simply belongs to that.

108792.explanation: Care must be accessible to everyone. The municipality should not discriminate based on faith or origin. Linking care to religion or culture emphasizes differences. The VVD believes that quality of care should always come first.

108793.explanation: The costs of youth care must be reduced and the care must be of better quality. The VVD therefore wants better management of the care the municipality purchases, and also looks at the price.

108794.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to provide menstrual products. The VVD encourages social initiatives that contribute to making menstrual products available at schools or other locations.

108797.explanation: The VVD does not want to increase the burdens on entrepreneurs. A higher tourist tax may cause fewer people to visit Almere and hamper the economy. Entrepreneurs would be the victims of that.

108800.explanation: The VVD does not want to use agricultural land for solar panels. Solar panels are better placed on roofs of houses, schools, offices, and other suitable buildings.

108802.explanation: The VVD considers an outdoor swimming pool at Bachweg in Almere Muziekwijk a poor choice. There is too little space, and the pool would be too small for the (future) size of our city.

108810.explanation: The VVD opposes the establishment of a shelter for addicts in Oosterwold. We are also against such a facility in Almere Poort. We want an integrated housing and care plan first before locations are designated.

108812.explanation: The Almere VVD wants a IJmeer connection between Almere and Amsterdam. Good accessibility by public transport and car is a requirement for housing development in Almere Pampus.

108813.explanation: VVD Almere supports a lively and vibrant city, which includes (large) events. We prefer that the organization of (large) events is done by market parties, not by the municipality. The municipality's role is to facilitate this and simplify regulations.

108752.explanation: A referendum is fine, but the principles must be clear and transparent to avoid later disappointment.

108756.explanation: An asylum seeker center (AZC) in your municipality increases pressure on various issues in the social domain, healthcare, and ultimately also the pressure on housing supply.

108761.explanation: Here too, money is spent that residents have paid via taxes. Not everyone is happy about that.

108764.explanation: An entrepreneur is personally responsible for that, not the municipality.

108769.explanation: First conduct research into the underlying cause. Then see what can be done with the research results. Then a decision can be made.

108770.explanation: For a young city like Almere, history is important. Attention to this is important.

108771.explanation: Safety has been a neglected aspect for years in the growing city of Almere.

108772.explanation: If you have nothing to hide, you have nothing to fear about being caught on camera.

108774.explanation: Unfortunately, it is a necessary evil that BOAs must protect themselves or need protection to enforce rules. A baton can be a useful tool in this.

108775.explanation: Experiments with this are already underway; first let’s wait for the results before we continue or stop.

108778.explanation: There is scarcity in the housing market, and an obligation to live there is one way to prevent speculation.

108779.explanation: Almere chooses a strategic approach to select the right locations and monitor the impact on the neighborhood, with attention to financial feasibility for developers.

108781.explanation: But no projects that stand in the way of real jobs. And it can support finding regular employment.

108782.explanation: However, a study on the effectiveness of debt forgiveness is appropriate.

108783.explanation: There are sufficient schemes alongside the national standard.

108785.explanation: This is too much freedom without adequately facing the financial consequences.

108789.explanation: This can (optionally) be included in the new public transport tender.

108790.explanation: The city remains freely accessible to everyone. Almere belongs to the people of Almere, not to climate activists.

108791.explanation: Parking permits cost money (including the system) and thus constitute an additional burden for residents.

108792.explanation: No religious exceptions in municipal policy. Almere must be a community, not a collection of parallel societies.

108793.explanation: Quality/price ratio is important. It is better to enforce more on healthcare fraud.

108794.explanation: Unnecessary, because local arrangements (De Schoor) already exist for this.

108797.explanation: Almere is not high on the tourist ladder. It is therefore important for Almere to increase the number of visits, which is not made more attractive by raising the rate.

108800.explanation: Agricultural land in Almere is scarce and much land is already designated for housing. A solar field locks up the area for years (25 years).

108802.explanation: The PVV questions the chosen location: is it feasible, affordable, and realistic?

108810.explanation: Almere is already fully occupied with the current amenities that exist in the city.

108812.explanation: It is 'cash on the barrelhead' in Pampus; Almere also builds for the Randstad, where good connections are a prerequisite.

108813.explanation: The council inquiry into the Floriade brutally exposed what the PVV has always warned about. The project was a financial fiasco from day one. Mistake after mistake was made, and warnings were repeatedly ignored. The management learned nothing from Omniworld.

108752.explanation: D66 values that residents not only think along but actively co-decide. Referenda fit within a broader movement towards more direct democracy, where residents help determine the city's direction. Referenda make complex choices transparent.

108756.explanation: People fleeing are not in a luxurious position but in an emergency. D66 Almere always chooses humanity and realism over superficial solutions. Closing an asylum seeker center does not solve anything: people do not disappear but end up homeless in parks, near stations, or in illegal situations.

108761.explanation: Fireworks will be banned nationally from 2026. A central fireworks show in each district creates a safe environment where families can celebrate New Year’s Eve together. Shows can be designed sustainably: with light, lasers, and sound, less fine dust, and less impact on animals.

108764.explanation: Accessibility is a right. D66 Almere believes that people with disabilities must be able to participate. There are still many obstacles in public spaces and buildings. The municipality can support entrepreneurs with advice and subsidies. The VIP group can help with this as well.

108769.explanation: Sports clubs are community anchors. They offer health, connection, rhythm, and safety. D66 believes it is important that everyone can play sports regardless of income. Without subsidies to clubs, sports would become unaffordable for many.

108770.explanation: Almere is young, but history is not only antiquity. Architecture, landscapes, artworks, and buildings deserve protection. They strengthen Almere’s identity and pride. Tourism in turn is an important engine for our local economy.

108771.explanation: BOAs are a visible symbol of safety and proximity for Almere. Where police capacity is structurally under pressure, BOAs are essential to safeguard livability and safety. It is not only about enforcement but about presence. At the same time, we invest in prevention.

108772.explanation: Cameras can be useful for evidence and investigation in places with structural crime, but large-scale camera surveillance undermines privacy and can lead to normalization of a surveillance state. D66 opts for a democratic balance: cameras only where there is substantiated need, and temporarily.

108774.explanation: Arming BOAs fundamentally changes their role: from mediator to law enforcer with means of violence. That can increase escalation rather than reduce it. We want to first invest in proper training, (mental) support, communication skills, and bodycams.

108775.explanation: The housing market systematically excludes starters. A starter’s loan increases their financing options and strengthens attachment: talent stays in Almere, families build neighborhoods, and the support base for schools and clubs grows. D66 sees housing as a right, not a commodity.

108778.explanation: Homes are meant to live in, not to make quick money. Investors push home seekers out of the market, make neighborhoods anonymous, and drive up prices. A self-occupancy requirement protects affordability and community building.

108779.explanation: Why new construction when thousands of square meters stand empty? Converting offices saves time, money, and materials and makes neighborhoods lively. Empty buildings cause decay and create a sense of insecurity. Almere can add housing faster for middle incomes and starters this way.

108781.explanation: D66 believes it is important that everyone can participate as much and wherever possible. People on social assistance more often face mental or health problems due to loneliness and financial stress. Therefore, D66 chooses guidance, education, and tailored pathways towards (volunteer) work.

108782.explanation: Debt remission prevents the buildup of debts and societal costs. It is cheaper and better to prevent people from falling into a pit than to help them climb out. People simply cannot afford the burdens. D66 chooses security of existence, not poverty punishment.

108783.explanation: More and more people sit just above the standard but can barely afford groceries, healthcare, or energy. By relaxing limits, we prevent new poverty and the accumulation of debts. Ultimately, prevention is always cheaper than fixing afterwards.

108785.explanation: A pilot basic income tests existential security, mental peace, and willingness to work. A basic income is not nationally regulated and therefore cannot yet be widely introduced in municipalities. Almere dares to pioneer with innovative policies. By carefully evaluating results, Almere can share experience.

108789.explanation: Neighborhood cohesion depends on good mobility. D66 chooses public transport, shared bikes, and electric shuttles to strengthen freedom and connectedness. Besides, a strong public transport and bicycle network is good for climate and environment in our city.

108790.explanation: A generous transition period is planned and will be maintained so every resident has time and space to get used to it and prepare. In the center, air quality is poor due to all the car traffic. An environmental zone improves air quality.

108791.explanation: D66 Almere sees that parking pressure harms livability in some neighborhoods. In these situations, a parking permit can be a good instrument to restore calm and fair use. A generic introduction of parking permits is not suitable for all neighborhoods. Therefore, D66 chooses a tailored approach.

108792.explanation: D66 Almere finds it important that people feel safe, understood, and respected in care. Attention to culture and personal values can contribute to better care and trust between caregiver and client. At the same time, organizing care solely based on culture is not realistic.

108793.explanation: Youth care focuses on vulnerable children and families. For D66 Almere, price should never be the main criterion when purchasing youth assistance. Moreover, there are clear national price guidelines. When costs become leading, quality comes under pressure.

108794.explanation: Period poverty increases inequality and leads to shame, medical risks, and school absenteeism. No one should have to improvise with toilet paper or towels. Therefore, D66 wants products to be freely available in schools, community centers, and public libraries.

108797.explanation: Almere is not a mass tourist city but wants to develop further as an attractive destination for events and recreation. Too high a tourist tax can actually hold this back. D66 chooses a balanced approach: a reasonable rate that contributes to facilities.

108800.explanation: The energy transition requires space. Solar panels on agricultural land can contribute if carefully integrated and combined with nature development or dual use. D66 Almere prioritizes solar panels on roofs but does not fundamentally exclude ground installations.

108802.explanation: D66 finds swimming facilities important for health, social connection, and swimming safety. The current pools are outdated and urgently need renewal. Moreover, not every city district has a pool. D66 wants to be the first to allocate funds for this.

108810.explanation: Distributing addiction care is necessary for safety, the well-being of those needing care, livability, and support in our city. Site selection requires care, good communication, and guidance. D66 carefully weighs the impact on surroundings per location.

108812.explanation: Good public transport connections are crucial for accessibility and sustainability. D66 believes large-scale area development is only responsible with high-quality public transport connections. Therefore, D66 clearly chooses: Pampus only with excellent public transport and strong bicycle connections.

108813.explanation: Events can contribute to pride, economy, and culture. D66 finds it very important to learn from past mistakes. So no repetition of errors, careful preparation and consideration by implementing an extensive large projects regulation.

108752.explanation: We consider a referendum a suitable instrument for major themes. Think of expensive and large events and projects, such as the Floriade in the past.

108756.explanation: Almere currently contributes its share to national reception efforts. We consider this a good thing. However, we are not in favor of expansion and believe other municipalities should first contribute more.

108761.explanation: It is important to offer an alternative to private fireworks that are banned starting this year. A fireworks, drone, or light show is a good alternative.

108764.explanation: Entrepreneurs are responsible for this themselves. The municipality must ensure that public spaces and municipal buildings are accessible to everyone, including people with disabilities.

108769.explanation: We believe the municipality should be able to help and contribute under certain conditions and circumstances.

108770.explanation: Monuments often have great emotional value for residents. Think of the Forest of the Unyielding. Other monuments contribute to Almere's identity, such as the Footnote.

108771.explanation: The SP has already ensured the number of neighborhood enforcement officers (BOAs) is expanded and that BOAs are employed directly by the municipality (previously they worked for a commercial security company). We want to further invest in more BOAs and enforcers, especially more neighborhood BOAs, in the coming years!

108772.explanation: We want to expand camera surveillance in the center and entertainment districts. We want to reverse the earlier budget cuts on camera surveillance.

108774.explanation: BOAs must be well equipped to safely perform their duties. If Almere's BOAs indicate they need this, we will equip them with batons and pepper spray.

108775.explanation: The housing shortage is one of the biggest challenges at the moment. We must do everything to ensure everyone has a roof over their head.

108778.explanation: The housing shortage is significant. Everyone who buys a home must actually live in it.

108779.explanation: This is the task of project developers. The municipality must facilitate this and make it easier by quickly processing permit applications.

108781.explanation: Social assistance serves to support people temporarily. They should be guided back to paid work as soon as possible. When this is (medically) not possible, we must consider how these people can contribute to society in other ways.

108782.explanation: To prevent people from falling into deeper financial trouble, debt remission must always remain possible and even be applied automatically. Financial problems cause much suffering and cost the municipality much more in support than debt remission.

108783.explanation: Many working poor live just above the threshold yet cannot make ends meet despite having a job. The municipality must actively support this group as well. Extra attention should be paid to combating child poverty.

108785.explanation: The SP supports well-paid work. Working must always pay off.

108789.explanation: The SP wants to further invest in regular city bus connections. These must be accessible to everyone.

108790.explanation: According to the RIVM, 12,000 people die prematurely annually in the Netherlands due to air pollution. The air in Almere's center is also poor quality. To protect the health of Almere residents living downtown, we must ban polluting old vehicles.

108791.explanation: We only want to introduce this in neighborhoods where a majority of residents indicates a need for it. Think of residential neighborhoods around the center where there is a lot of nuisance from shoppers parking in adjacent neighborhoods.

108792.explanation: Care providers and patients must respect each other's beliefs and culture. We live in a free country where everyone may think and believe what they want.

108793.explanation: Price and quality must always be balanced. Poor quality often leads to higher costs.

108794.explanation: The SP believes the municipality should provide free tampons and sanitary pads to women living below the poverty line. There must be no taboo surrounding menstruation. This way, we prevent women from being unable to participate in society, work, and school during their period.

108797.explanation: The SP believes that visitors from outside who use the facilities, roads, and waterways in our city should also make a fair financial contribution. Currently, taxes in Amsterdam are much higher, causing many tourists to stay overnight here in Almere. They too must contribute.

108800.explanation: The SP believes we should not install solar panels on land but should focus on stimulating the installation of solar panels on the roofs of homes, businesses, and municipal buildings. This way, the energy can be used directly where it is generated.

108802.explanation: The SP promised in the previous election program that there would be an outdoor swimming pool. We have kept this promise. Currently, plans are being prepared for a pool in the Music District. We continue to support the construction but will closely monitor the costs. The costs must not escalate.

108810.explanation: An additional addiction shelter to alleviate the nuisance caused by the shelter in the city center is necessary. Oosterwold is less densely populated than the rest of the city and is therefore more suitable than other locations. However, the municipality, police, and care providers must guarantee safety in Oosterwold.

108812.explanation: A new traffic connection with Amsterdam is necessary to prevent traffic jams in Almere Poort and the rest of the city.

108813.explanation: The SP has opposed money-draining prestige projects for years. For example, we opposed the Floriade and ensured in 2025 that most of the budget for the 50th anniversary was cut. We also prevented an exorbitantly expensive megalomaniac art museum.

108752.explanation: Almere is not a company, and residents are not customers. Many residents want to have a say in their city or neighborhood, and their involvement is becoming increasingly important. The municipality carefully weighs initiatives, plans, or concerns from residents when making decisions.

108756.explanation: The municipality has a humane shelter policy for people seeking safe accommodation and treats them equally. Almere offers small-scale shelter to refugees spread throughout the municipality by making agreements with the COA. Almere will continue to maintain a generous share in shelter provision.

108761.explanation: Festive, safe, and environmentally friendly alternatives to fireworks, such as shows with light, music, drones, and lasers, can take place as long as people, animals, nature, and the environment do not suffer any harm.

108764.explanation: The municipality facilitates entrepreneurs where possible by, for example, granting permits to make their buildings more accessible. The entrepreneur bears these costs themselves.

108769.explanation: Sports and physical activity hold an important place in our municipality. Sports promote our physical and mental health. Sports also have a social function, and sports clubs play an important role in this. The municipality rents locations to sports clubs at cost price.

108770.explanation: Almere has very few municipal monuments. The monuments that exist, such as the Voetnoot or the Bivak, must be cherished and well maintained. The demolition of (cultural) historical and valuable buildings must be prevented.

108771.explanation: There will be more focus on the causes of crime and its prevention, for example by investing in young people in disadvantaged situations. The municipality also emphasizes prevention through good and meaningful youth work and sufficient community workers, social workers, neighborhood parents, and district police officers.

108772.explanation: Privacy concerns lead to very cautious use of camera surveillance in public spaces. Camera surveillance offers only a false sense of security and often only causes the problem to be displaced. Automatic facial recognition is too intrusive and will not be used.

108774.explanation: Expanding the monopoly on violence does not make Almere safer. This must remain with the police.

108775.explanation: Starter loans have a price-inflating effect and are therefore not favorable in the long term. The municipality ensures at least 40% social housing, affordable mid-range rent up to €1000, and plans for truly affordable starter homes for first-time buyers.

108778.explanation: Houses are meant to be lived in, not a business model. With an owner-occupancy obligation, investors are kept out of neighborhoods, preventing cheap homes from being bought up and rented out at high prices.

108779.explanation: Almere enforces the vacancy ordinance, makes vacant buildings habitable again, and urges the national government to legalize squatting of long-term vacant buildings again, while not enforcing against it themselves.

108781.explanation: The municipality supports benefit recipients in doing volunteer work, informal care, and internships. Even if these activities are not directly aimed at returning to the labor market and without making volunteering mandatory.

108782.explanation: Municipal taxes are more readily waived or taken over, among other things, to prevent poverty traps. The asset limit standards for municipal tax remission and the personal contribution for the Social Support Act (Wmo) are aligned at 130% of the social minimum.

108783.explanation: Almere expands the minimum income policy to incomes up to 130% of the social minimum. This includes, for example, exceptional benefits and collective health insurance.

108785.explanation: The introduction of a basic income promotes more equality. The municipality indicates to the national government, which is responsible for this, that Almere wants to conduct a pilot with a basic income.

108789.explanation: The car is no longer dominant, and Almere puts pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport at the center.

108790.explanation: Polluting traffic is excluded by extending the environmental zone to the entire built-up area. We will continue implementing the zero-emission zone for commercial traffic in city centers.

108791.explanation: Almere chooses to reclaim public space from cars. By fully focusing on more modes of transportation than just cars, our space becomes greener and healthier, and we also take necessary steps to combat transport poverty.

108792.explanation: The Party for the Animals considers personal freedom a great good. Freedom of choice based on any conviction fits this. Unfortunately, this will not always be possible in practice due to shortages in healthcare.

108793.explanation: High-quality youth care is sometimes (literally) a matter of life and death. Good (preventive) youth care prevents problems in later life. In the past, cheap solutions have often proved costly.

108794.explanation: Menstrual products are essential hygiene items to which access is not always self-evident.

108797.explanation: Tourism causes significant damage to the climate and nature through accompanying polluting transport and overconsumption. Therefore, the municipality uses tourist tax to promote sustainability and strengthen nature to limit the damage from tourism. Raising the tax is not a goal in itself.

108800.explanation: Agricultural land is not intended for solar parks but for plant-based food production and nature development and, if livestock farming shrinks significantly, for housing.

108802.explanation: The proposed swimming pool costs the municipality a lot of money while having a small capacity. This money would be better invested in, for example, improving water quality throughout Almere and better facilities on the beaches.

108810.explanation: It is important to spread different care facilities throughout the city and not concentrate them in one location. The Party for the Animals prefers to refer to "people with an addiction" rather than "addicts."

108812.explanation: Due to the housing crisis, it is important to quickly build sufficient homes. For this, sufficient facilities and good accessibility are very important. We do not support a new motorway. We prefer an IJmeer connection with metro and bicycle.

108813.explanation: We believe Almere should be very cautious about engaging in new large projects and other major developments where much is promised but much is also uncertain. Municipal finances are under pressure, and trust in politics is low.

108761.explanation: This could become a very nice tradition. Then the residents can decide whether to participate.

108769.explanation: We find clubs very important, but we believe support can take more forms than just financial support.

108770.explanation: Neutral. We hardly have any monuments, so we can certainly take care of that.

108771.explanation: Because safety also means combating illegal dumping, nuisance in neighborhoods, and street intimidation.

108772.explanation: At least easily (temporarily) deployable at locations currently considered unsafe.

108774.explanation: Neutral. A baton is not needed for many tasks that a BOA has.

108752.explanation: A binding referendum is a precondition for a well-functioning local democracy.

108756.explanation: We want to close all shelter locations in the municipality.

108761.explanation: In a municipality like Almere, a fireworks show can be very nice, but this should not replace the traditional fireworks tradition where residents set off fireworks themselves.

108769.explanation: Associations have an important social and community function, and deserve support when it is truly necessary.

108770.explanation: There are no buildings in Almere with monumental value, so this money can be well used for other purposes.

108771.explanation: We advocate for more fully qualified police officers, but as an interim measure, enforcement officers (boa's) can offer a solution. These boa's should only have security tasks and should not be used as fine-issuing machines.

108775.explanation: We want to support young people and first-time buyers in many ways to purchase a home, but we believe that a starter loan is not the solution for this.

108781.explanation: Reciprocal obligations encourage participation in society and ensure that a safety net truly remains a safety net.

108782.explanation: We want to first significantly reduce the local tax burden for residents before considering such a measure.

108783.explanation: The current income threshold is appropriate.

108785.explanation: We see no merit in a basic income and do not want the municipality to engage in such experiments.

108789.explanation: Public transportation in Almere is very well arranged. A measure like a demand-responsive bus service is unnecessary.

108790.explanation: We are absolutely against an environmental zone. This would be disastrous for accessibility, freedom of movement, and the local economy.

108791.explanation: We want to maintain freedom to park.

108792.explanation: We support freedom of choice in healthcare. People should always be able to choose a doctor who suits their preferences, within the limits of the law and our norms and values.

108794.explanation: We see no reason for the municipality to do this. It is a personal responsibility.

108797.explanation: We see no immediate reason to raise this tax now, but we are not fundamentally opposed if it could help fill budget gaps.

108800.explanation: We are generally against the development of solar parks.

108802.explanation: We find this location unsuitable. It is too small and will have too much negative impact on the surrounding environment.

108810.explanation: We do not want to establish nuisance-causing shelters in, next to, or near residential neighborhoods, including Oosterwold.

108812.explanation: Good accessibility by car must be a basic requirement for the development of Almere Pampus. The IJmeer connection must be a car connection.

108813.explanation: The municipality has shown that it cannot manage such large projects. This should be left to the market.

108752.explanation: Referendums reduce complex decisions to a yes or no choice and do not do justice to the complexity of many proposals. Referendums increase and sharpen societal divisions instead of producing solutions that satisfy many people.

108756.explanation: Almere must contribute to the reception of refugees. We take responsibility in accordance with the distribution law.

108761.explanation: The CDA supports fireworks shows in various neighborhoods and districts organized by fireworks associations that every Almere resident aged 18 and older can join.

108764.explanation: The entrepreneur is primarily responsible for the accessibility of their own business.

108769.explanation: The CDA values the community life of associations highly for the Almere community. If necessary, the municipality can financially support associations under conditions as a last resort.

108770.explanation: Almere is a young city with few to no monuments. According to the CDA, this has no priority.

108771.explanation: Safety in Almere is under pressure, and we see an important role for boa's in this.

108772.explanation: In business parks, for example, there is a growing need for camera surveillance to combat undermining crime within the legal privacy frameworks.

108774.explanation: There is a shortage of police officers in Almere. Partly, this is compensated by boa's. However, boa's must also have the means to properly carry out their duties. This could possibly include a baton.

108775.explanation: To make the housing market more accessible for Almere starters, this is a possible instrument under certain conditions.

108778.explanation: Through a motion by the CDA, this was already implemented in 2022, and the CDA still supports it, of course.

108779.explanation: This is a good opportunity to create additional housing, but it should take place as much as possible without subsidies.

108781.explanation: Meaningful social work ensures that these people remain part of the community and make a valuable contribution, which the CDA considers important. However, the statement suggests that social assistance recipients lose their benefits if they do not do this. We oppose that.

108782.explanation: Remission applies only to people who truly cannot afford to pay. The CDA sees no reason to stop this.

108783.explanation: Net disposable income should be considered when determining eligibility for assistance.

108785.explanation: Recent studies show that a basic income has no positive effect on prosperity, health, and happiness. Almere can spend its money more usefully.

108789.explanation: The CDA believes this is not a municipal task given the extensive public transport network and WMO provisions. However, the CDA supports private initiatives to improve mobility for, for example, the elderly.

108790.explanation: The CDA opposes the introduction of an environmental zone in the city center because it harms the competitive position and imposes costs on residents of the center. Moreover, the Almere city center is designed in such a way that an environmental zone is not needed for better air quality.

108791.explanation: Introducing this would only be aimed at generating more money for the municipality. A permit does not solve a problem.

108792.explanation: The CDA is grateful to everyone working in healthcare and providing services. There are significant shortages. It is undesirable if vulnerable groups can exclude certain service providers.

108793.explanation: Quality takes precedence over price. Good youth care can ultimately reduce costs. Of course, costs should be kept under control. This can be achieved, for example, through a more regional approach.

108794.explanation: We find a generic measure inappropriate, but we wholeheartedly support initiatives at schools and sports clubs to provide tampons and sanitary pads.

108797.explanation: The CDA wants to increase the number of tourists in Almere, therefore an increase in the tourist tax is undesirable.

108800.explanation: The CDA supports solar panels on roofs, not on the ground.

108802.explanation: The CDA considers the Bachweg an unsuitable location for an outdoor swimming pool. Locations on the edge of the city forest, the Olympus Park, or the Spanningsveld are much better places for an outdoor pool because there is much more space for recreation and play.

108810.explanation: Addicts must be evenly distributed across the city. Possible nuisance and insecurity must be prevented as much as possible.

If no motorway is built on the IJmeer connection, traffic pressure in Almere will quadruple. The CDA finds this unacceptable.

108813.explanation: There is no money for this, nor is there any support.

108752.explanation: DENK stands for more democracy, and a referendum is an excellent means to increase democratic say in important decisions.

108756.explanation: There must be sufficient humane shelter for refugees in our municipality. The municipality plays a crucial role in improving the reception and integration of refugees.

108761.explanation: We support a fireworks ban. The municipality should organize this so there is something that brings the community together during the year-end and fosters connection.

108764.explanation: Public spaces will be made more inclusive, with attention to accessibility for the elderly and people with disabilities.

108769.explanation: DENK sees sports as an essential means to prevent and address health problems. Sports must be accessible to all segments of the population, regardless of age, income, or background.

108770.explanation: Our monuments are there for a reason. We consider them important as a society, and this also entails responsibility to ensure the monuments look well maintained.

108771.explanation: Enforcement will be improved in disadvantaged neighborhoods.

108772.explanation: Camera surveillance is necessary to ensure safety but must not become an end in itself. It affects the privacy of our residents.

108774.explanation: BOAs should do the work of BOAs and not perform police tasks. The strength of BOAs lies precisely in that they do not have these kinds of repressive powers.

108775.explanation: We introduce an interest-free starter loan that complies with the principles of a halal mortgage. We are also exploring options to realize hire-purchase.

108778.explanation: To combat speculation, buyers in certain neighborhoods should be required to live in new-build homes themselves. This prevents homes from being immediately rented out and ensures they remain available for people who genuinely want to live there.

108779.explanation: To combat speculation, buyers in certain neighborhoods should be required to live in new-build homes themselves. This prevents homes from being immediately rented out and ensures they remain available for people who genuinely want to live there.

108781.explanation: A humane approach is central. Municipal rules that disproportionately interfere with citizens' lives, such as unreasonable sanctions in social assistance, must therefore be examined and abolished. The treatment of people on social assistance must be more humane. Provide guidance to employment.

108782.explanation: Tax increases that affect ordinary citizens should be avoided, especially for low and middle incomes. Exemptions for minimum-income earners will be maintained and increased where necessary. These arrangements will also be opened to self-employed entrepreneurs who need financial support.

108783.explanation: We want to invest more in social rental housing. More people will qualify for social housing. Income thresholds will be relaxed.

108785.explanation: Research shows that a basic income can contribute to improving livelihood security. DENK is open to a pilot in Almere.

108789.explanation: Investments in hydrogen buses and electric freight transport are encouraged. Electric buses and shared transport such as e-bikes and shared cars are explicitly promoted.

108790.explanation: The municipality of Almere does not implement environmental zones. These disproportionately affect citizens with limited financial means because they are more often forced to drive cheaper cars that generally emit more.

108791.explanation: We continue to fight against exorbitant parking fees. The car is not a cash cow. We understand that mobility is essential for working people and families.

108792.explanation: Culturally sensitive care becomes the norm, with training for care providers and policies that meet the diverse needs of our residents. Culturally sensitive care remains a top priority. Cultural sensitivity will be a requirement in healthcare procurement, and training in cultural sensitivity will be promoted.

108793.explanation: We make every effort to reduce waiting lists in youth care. Financial challenges will only increase in coming years due to declining national funding. Therefore, we allocate the necessary budget and focus more on prevention.

108794.explanation: It is a basic necessity; if Almere residents cannot afford it, the municipality must provide it.

108797.explanation: Municipal charges are distributed fairly. We protect vulnerable groups in this. Large polluters and multinationals can make a fairer contribution. The same applies to tourists.

108800.explanation: We massively commit to cleaner energy. We want to invest in solar and offshore wind energy. Coal-fired power plants must disappear as quickly as possible. We do not rule out nuclear energy because it is cleaner than other energy sources. Safe storage of nuclear waste is a strict precondition for any option.

108802.explanation: As Almere grows, facilities must also grow. From this perspective, we support making a new swimming pool possible.

108810.explanation: According to DENK, addiction care does not belong in residential neighborhoods. Oosterwold is a green residential area. Although we believe everyone in need should be helped, we must also consider residents and not harm their living environment.

108812.explanation: The development of Almere Pampus must continue, with a fully-fledged IJmeer connection including a motorway as a requirement.

108813.explanation: Almere is one of the larger cities in the Netherlands; large events put the city on the map. Almere is growing rapidly, which also creates a need for such events.

108752.explanation: The opinion of the residents is something a municipal council and city administration should be as informed about as possible. In our view, a referendum should be consultative, not decisive.

108756.explanation: First and foremost, the municipality must comply with the law. So closing is not an option. Moreover: where do we want to put all those people then? On the street? Unfortunately, there are already enough people wandering there...

108761.explanation: Especially now that other fireworks are banned (although we wonder if this ban will hold).

108764.explanation: It helps people with disabilities, whom we believe should have good access everywhere. We also provide subsidies to those people themselves for their own homes or rental housing.

108769.explanation: Sports (and also cultural development) are an essential part of social cohesion. Financial setbacks at a sports association affect hundreds of people at the same time in their enjoyment of life. The municipality must help where it can.

108770.explanation: We hardly have any monuments in our city, but ensuring beautiful (though that is a matter of taste) buildings is not an unnecessary luxury for a city that seriously wants to work on its foundation.

108774.explanation: Although it can also give a feeling of false security, we find that acting unarmed against aggression is not responsible.

108775.explanation: It is not the municipality's role to provide this. The former premium-A system did not come from the municipality either. It can also have a negative magnet effect on people from other cities.

108781.explanation: Those who can work are already activated through work, education, or socially useful activities. Note: people must also be able to do this, and such an activity must also suit the person performing it.

108782.explanation: Attempting to collect those taxes then really costs more than the waiver itself. What we do believe is that others besides benefit recipients (small entrepreneurs, for example) should also qualify.

108783.explanation: We interpret that as help in general terms. Help for those who need it must always be accessible; support should not actually depend on strict rules and income limits. There must always be room for customization.

108785.explanation: Although we were in favor of this in the past and closely monitor developments, we find experimenting with this idea in the next four years too risky. Considering the precarious financial situation and the problems yet to come.

108789.explanation: Affordable public transport and tailor-made transport must be arranged where necessary; Wmo and target group transport must be well and reliably organized.

108790.explanation: We do not want (zero) emission zones because the results do not outweigh the inconvenience, such a measure excludes many people who depend on an (older) car. The infrastructure will fall short and adequate alternatives are lacking.

108791.explanation: We do not want an expansion of paid parking or permit requirements without demonstrable necessity. The (movement) freedom of our residents is further restricted with every new rule.

108792.explanation: Freedom of choice yes, but no exclusive care or living groups based on culture or religion. That can exclude population groups and may resemble discrimination. Moreover, care already struggles with major staff and financial problems.

108793.explanation: Quality, effectiveness, and results take precedence over price in youth care. Cheap often turns out to be expensive here. Ten caregivers for one child is something we consider rather a budget to cut.

108794.explanation: Poverty aid must be targeted and effective, not symbolic politics that sounds attractive during election time.

108797.explanation: That has already increased significantly. We are scared that even higher tourist tax will deter visitors. It also discourages entrepreneurs who want to start something touristic in our city.

108800.explanation: As long as the electricity grid is overloaded, more solar fields make no sense anyway. Furthermore, they are bad for the environment, especially when damaged. Besides, we simply need our (agricultural) land.

108802.explanation: We are in favor of a good outdoor swimming pool, but the location on Bachweg is totally unsuitable for a new outdoor pool. The area is too small and the number of possible parking spaces utterly insufficient. The nuisance for residents would become irresponsibly high.

108810.explanation: Where various districts already contribute, we think Oosterwold should do so too. Shelters (and related problems) must be fairly spread across the city. Together the benefits, so also together the burdens.

108812.explanation: Development of Pampus may only begin under the strict condition that a full IJmeer connection with public transport AND a road is constructed.

108813.explanation: The municipality should not organize events; this should be left to expert organizations and entrepreneurs. The municipality may at most facilitate and should primarily focus on the residents of Almere.

108752.explanation: We at Sterk Lokaal Almere are absolutely in favor of this.

108756.explanation: Victims of wars are welcome for a certain period, but economic refugees are not.

108761.explanation: It has been impossible for years to properly enforce against illegal fireworks, let alone with a total ban; a fireworks show costs the municipality money it does not have, so why a ban at all?

108764.explanation: In principle, a property owner is responsible for this when it concerns renting, and if it is ownership, then it simply comes with doing business.

108769.explanation: One association is not the other (social character), this must be examined case by case and therefore requires customization.

108770.explanation: Maintain yes, but no extra money should go there if it is not strictly necessary.

108771.explanation: This is one of our spearheads under the theme of safety.

108772.explanation: This is for the safety of Almere residents.

108774.explanation: This should be standard equipment for the enforcement officers who patrol the streets.

108775.explanation: The municipality does not have authority over this.

108778.explanation: Agreed, given the tightness in the current housing market, there is no room in our opinion for speculative buying.

108779.explanation: We don't care how this happens as long as it happens.

108781.explanation: Stimulating is fine in our opinion, but mandating something is coercion and then resembles forced labor.

108782.explanation: It is a dilemma because others would have to pay more if those with low incomes were exempted.

108783.explanation: We will not comply with that, as it is legally regulated.

108785.explanation: As Strong Local Almere, we support this and thorough feasibility research must be conducted.

108789.explanation: This could be a solution for rural areas.

108790.explanation: Excluding types of transport means excluding people. Almere was built on a certain ideology, and that includes every form of mobility. In other words, HELL NO!!!

108791.explanation: The municipality must simply provide sufficient parking facilities.

108792.explanation: There are enormous care shortages; if you already receive care, you should be grateful that it exists.

108793.explanation: The focus should be on the right and most appropriate care, not primarily on money. We also choose assistance close to home.

108794.explanation: The statement suggests it is a must, but in our perception, this is not a sacred obligation.

108797.explanation: Tourists can certainly pay a bit more tax. This is an increase that does not come at the expense of Almere residents.

108800.explanation: Agricultural land must remain agricultural land.

108802.explanation: Not in the middle of a residential area; a better choice would be near the top sports complex due to accessibility.

108810.explanation: If other districts have this, it should simply also be possible in Oosterwold. Why spare Oosterwold?

108812.explanation: This has been the intention for years; without that connection, there simply is no Pampus. It is a municipal council decision and a provincial decision.

108813.explanation: No major projects for Almere in the next 10 years. First, get the finances in order.

108752.explanation: Citizen participation is highly valued by JA21, and a referendum is part of that. JA21 wants to return influence to the citizens, especially in Almere.

108756.explanation: At the national level, necessary measures must be taken to reduce asylum seeker inflow to an absolute minimum. JA21 Almere will continue to oppose the implementation of the Distribution Act and any expansion of the AZC in Almere, the English Mile.

108764.explanation: That is primarily the responsibility of the entrepreneur and is not part of the municipality’s core tasks.

108769.explanation: Primarily, sports clubs are always responsible for their own financial health, and there is no role for the municipality. Depending on the situation and social importance, tailor-made solutions may only be possible in exceptional cases.

108770.explanation: Monument care, guarding and maintaining the heritage of our young city, is important and can count on support from JA21.

108771.explanation: Safety is an absolute priority for JA21. We are the 7th largest city in the Netherlands but rank 17th in safety expenditures. On average, we manage well with incidents, but the rising trend is very worrying. Additional municipal enforcers are an absolute necessity.

108772.explanation: The preventive effect of camera surveillance should be part of the street safety package JA21 wants to implement. We are definitely in favor of using this instrument.

108774.explanation: The expansion of equipment with weapons deserves careful attention. With increasing aggression and in line with the BOA ACP union and the Dutch Boa Bond, we are open to supporting this expansion.

108775.explanation: In larger cities like Almere, JA21 prioritizes building owner-occupied homes for starters and middle incomes. Improving the financial position of starters through starter loans can contribute to this. The focus remains building instead of subsidies.

108778.explanation: We do not support a self-occupancy obligation. JA21 stimulates homeownership and does not want to punish or discourage it. The housing crisis must be tackled by building, promoting homeownership, and reducing hindering housing market regulations.

108779.explanation: Housing shortage is high and demands quick, pragmatic solutions. We can support a fast conversion of suitable commercial buildings into housing locations as part of expanding the housing supply.

108781.explanation: With social assistance benefits, you should still be expected to contribute to society. Requesting a counter-performance will certainly be applicable for JA21.

108782.explanation: Municipal services (which should be more limited to core tasks) are for all Almere citizens, from whom a contribution is expected.

108783.explanation: We see no possibility to adjust this scheme. Further raising the threshold would make it unaffordable and unsustainable.

108785.explanation: It is better to strengthen the economy, create jobs, and transition from a commuter town to a full-fledged city. Municipal financing of a basic income could lead to tax increases, which we definitely do not support.

108789.explanation: Almere was designed with very good infrastructure, with separate lanes for different traffic flows (bus, car, bicycle). Extra tailored transport would then be unnecessary.

108790.explanation: No patchwork of environmental zones, not in Almere either. No harassing cars, no restriction of access to city centers for residents, staff, freelancers, or visitors. Freedom of choice in mobility is very important to us.

108791.explanation: A parking permit does not solve the problem, nor does lowering the parking standard. This will only lead to more problems and rising costs for citizens, especially now that multiple generations have to live in one home because of the housing crisis. Are we going to tax them even more?

108792.explanation: Suitable, reliable, and quality care will always have to be the priority. Creating differences based on religion or culture can create barriers to accessible care, which JA21 finds undesirable and inappropriate.

108793.explanation: Price is an important factor in youth care decisions, but quality of care is equally important. Focusing solely on price is too one-sided. The effectiveness of youth care should be examined because parts of the current approach lead to unnecessarily high costs.

108794.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality, and there are already programs providing Menstrual Product Distribution Points (MUPs).

108797.explanation: Absolutely not. Almere already has the highest tourist tax in Flevoland, especially after the most recent increase. This limits Almere’s appeal and is not good for entrepreneurs or strengthening Almere’s economy.

108800.explanation: Agricultural land in Flevoland is considered among the most fertile and best farming areas in the world. We will not cover it with solar panels. More suitable locations can be chosen, such as roofs and coverings of large parking lots.

108802.explanation: Almere definitely needs an outdoor swimming pool, but one that is designed to accommodate the city's growth and can offer sufficient recreation and accessibility. This does not apply to the location on Bachweg.

108810.explanation: The research conducted so far, combined with a lack of genuine participation, gives JA21 no reason to support the shelter in Oosterwold (or Poort).

108812.explanation: JA21 sees this so-called full-fledged IJmeer connection as an absolute prerequisite for the new Almere Pampus district. Without additional access for this district, it will otherwise lead to unnecessary traffic congestion.

108813.explanation: Research has shown that an experiment like the Floriade should never have taken place. There have been several points of measurement to halt this, something the administration failed to do. However, there must be space for proven major events such as the Challenge Almere.

108752.explanation: If it really matters, the voices of residents should be directly weighed. Decisions affect you locally on your street and in your wallet. A referendum gives people ownership, builds trust, and forces politicians to explain better and listen. Democracy must be tangible.

108756.explanation: Generatie Almere chooses a humane approach. If you organize shelters well and manageably, you prevent chaos and distribute responsibility fairly. Almere has the facilities and shows that it can be done: clearly, safely, and with respect for both people and the city.

108761.explanation: Fireworks are part of the Netherlands. Generatie Almere regrets a general ban that is hardly enforceable. A fireworks show can be a solution if there is support.

108764.explanation: People with disabilities must be able to fully participate in society; however, in cases where the entrepreneur lacks the financial means to make this possible, the municipality can play a role.

108769.explanation: Generatie Almere supports a strong sports offer, but the municipality is not a safety net for every club that loses members. If something becomes less popular, that is sometimes a natural development. Municipal funds should go to broad sports facilities and youth, not to endlessly rescuing clubs.

108770.explanation: Almere has few monuments; precisely because of that, you must cherish them. Generatie Almere wants to keep them in good condition: what you let deteriorate now, you pay for doubly later. Monument care is not a luxury, but careful stewardship of the city's story and identity.

108771.explanation: Almere is growing, so enforcement must grow along. Generatie Almere wants more enforcement officers with specialties (youth, nuisance, traffic). The focus lies on safety and livability.

108772.explanation: Generatie Almere chooses safety with freedom. More cameras often provide mostly a false sense of security and shift problems elsewhere, while privacy disappears. Only cameras where it is truly necessary and demonstrably effective.

108774.explanation: Generatie Almere does not see this as the ultimate solution for safety, but as protection for enforcement officers. The baton should only be given under strict training and clear rules, solely for self-defense. This way, enforcement officers are not left empty-handed, without it becoming a power tool.

108775.explanation: Generatie Almere wants new construction to mainly help Almere residents. A starter loan can ensure that homes do not automatically go to outsiders but more often to people who live, work, and have built their lives here. This way, Almere truly remains a city for its residents.

108779.explanation: Generatie Almere does not want expensive subsidies for a solution that often yields little here. Vacancy and decay must be tackled: make conversion possible, set requirements, and speed up permits. But public money only if it demonstrably results in many homes.

108781.explanation: Generatie Almere does not want punishment or coercion, but participation. Meaningful social work helps with structure, self-worth, and contact, and makes the step to work smaller. So: with customization and support, as an incentive to be part of society again, not as an obligation to punish.

108782.explanation: Generatie Almere wants people with low income to be able to breathe. Forgiveness prevents small bills from becoming large debts. Especially if you have little, every euro helps to maintain peace and build something. Not punishing harder, but supporting smarter.

108783.explanation: Generatie Almere wants help not to stop just above the threshold: many people now fall between stools. Raise the income limit but mainly make help practical: advising, instructing, and where necessary monitoring, so that support truly helps and people move forward again.

108785.explanation: Research into a basic income can be local, but Generatie Almere wants Almere to first focus on the basics: housing, safety, and poverty approaches that already work. We don’t always have to be at the forefront with large experiments.

108789.explanation: Generatie Almere wants to invest in solid public transport. A call bus is a temporary fix: inconsistent, less predictable, and no basis for growth. Preferably invest money in fixed, reliable lines and better connections that work long-term.

108790.explanation: Generatie Almere does not want an environmental zone that affects residents and small entrepreneurs. Tackle pollution where it is greatest: heavy commercial vehicles and logistics. Encourage cleaner transport and smart provisioning, without making the center unaffordable.

108792.explanation: Agreed. Care truly works when you feel safe and understood. Generatie Almere believes people should be able to choose a caregiver that fits their faith, culture, or belief wherever possible. Appropriate care increases trust, cooperation, and recovery.

108793.explanation: Youth care is a major problem in Almere; you cannot primarily select based on price. Generatie Almere wants quality and reliability to weigh more so help is appropriate and stable. Price matters, but must never be the main choice.

108794.explanation: Agreed. Period poverty exists, even in Almere. Generatie Almere wants no one to stay home from school or work over something as basic as sanitary pads. Cheap dispensers at schools and public places help immediately, without hassle or shame.

108800.explanation: Generatie Almere does not want a rigid ban. Where possible, agricultural land can be used multifunctionally, with solar panels combined with cultivation or nature. This way you generate sustainable energy without unnecessarily excluding space.

108802.explanation: Generatie Almere currently finds a new swimming pool unrealistic. The location on Bachweg and the municipality’s financial room make this unwise at this moment. Prefer to focus resources on existing facilities and other urgent tasks.

108810.explanation: Agreed. Generatie Almere finds Oosterwold more suitable than Poort or the center. The calm, green environment helps recovery and prevents nuisance in busy residential areas. In Oosterwold there is space for a full care complex, not just a building among homes.

108812.explanation: Generatie Almere does want a metro connection but no new highway. Almost no one supports the car connection. A metro strengthens public transport for the whole of Almere and connects Pampus sustainably with the city and Amsterdam. Invest in a strong tram and metro network in Almere, not extra asphalt.

108813.explanation: Generatie Almere believes the Floriade showed that large-scale events without a realistic revenue model or self-sufficiency give little to the city and actually entail risks. Such projects must be critically assessed in the future.

108752.explanation: Residents must be able to have direct influence on major decisions. Referenda strengthen local democracy, provided they are used carefully and well explained. This way we make policy together with residents, not over their heads.

108756.explanation: Asylum shelter is a shared responsibility. Almere continues to contribute to humane shelter, with attention to support and distribution. Sending people in need away does not fit with a just and solidary city.

108761.explanation: Together for Almere chooses safety, livability, and sustainability. A fireworks show can unite and be festive but brings risks and environmental impact. Therefore a balanced approach: safe, limited, and sustainable, possibly with alternative celebrations.

108764.explanation: Accessibility is a basic right. The municipality must financially support entrepreneurs in making adaptations so that people with disabilities can participate everywhere. This is fair and good for the local economy.

108769.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for health and social cohesion. If they get into trouble, the municipality must step in to maintain their social role.

108770.explanation: Heritage tells the city’s story. Cutting back on monument care means loss of identity and history. Careful preservation is a public responsibility.

108771.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and trust in neighborhoods. Enforcement officers are an important link between residents, police, and municipality. Prevention and proximity are central.

108772.explanation: Cameras can help in specific locations but are no miracle solution. Always combined with supervision, good lighting, and social measures, with respect for privacy.

108774.explanation: Enforcement officers must be able to work safely, but arming them does not fit their role. Good training, cooperation with police, and prevention are more effective and proportionate.

108775.explanation: Starters barely stand a chance on the housing market. With starter loans, the municipality helps young people and families get a first home and strengthens ties to the city.

108778.explanation: Homes are to live in, not for speculation. Owner-occupancy requirements prevent investor buying and increase opportunities for starters and families.

108779.explanation: Transforming vacant offices into housing is sustainable and efficient. With targeted subsidies, this can be accelerated, especially for affordable homes.

108781.explanation: Together for Almere supports welfare recipients, but mandatory social work can be stigmatizing. Participation should be voluntary, appropriate, and focused on independence.

108782.explanation: Exemption from fees prevents people with low incomes from falling deeper into trouble. Ensuring basic security is paramount; poverty must not be increased by local taxes.

108783.explanation: Poverty also affects people just above the current threshold. By raising the income limit, more Almere residents receive timely support, preventing small problems from becoming major debts.

108785.explanation: Together for Almere is open to innovation but first chooses to strengthen basic security through targeted poverty policy and participation. A pilot can only proceed after careful consideration and coordination at the national level.

108789.explanation: Tailored transport increases accessibility for the elderly, people with disabilities, and residents without a car. This strengthens inclusion and participation in society.

108790.explanation: Clean air is important, but measures must be feasible and socially responsible. Together for Almere opts for gradual sustainability without excluding people or entrepreneurs.

108791.explanation: Parking pressure varies per neighborhood. Permits can help, but only where residents deem it necessary. Resident participation and customization are leading principles.

108792.explanation: Appropriate care increases trust and effectiveness. Freedom of choice within care options contributes to inclusion and respect for diversity.

108793.explanation: The quality and continuity of care are decisive. Cheap youth care that does not work ultimately costs more and harms children and families.

108794.explanation: Period poverty hinders participation at school and work. Making menstrual products freely available at public venues contributes to equality and health.

108797.explanation: Tourists may contribute to facilities, but any increase must be balanced and keep Almere attractive. Careful consideration is necessary.

108800.explanation: Sustainable energy is necessary, but agricultural land is scarce. Priority should be given to solar panels on roofs, with space for customization as long as nature and food production are not harmed.

108802.explanation: We support a new outdoor swimming pool, but not at Bachweg. Participation from local residents was insufficient; their concerns deserve serious attention. Moreover, this location offers little room for future growth.

108810.explanation: Shelter is needed, but location choice requires careful consideration and support. Together for Almere looks at distribution, accessibility, and safety.

108812.explanation: Pampus offers opportunities for large-scale housing construction. The start of the first phase (approximately 7,500 homes) can proceed with facilities and temporary mobility solutions, even without an immediate car and metro connection to Amsterdam.

108813.explanation: Almere should no longer organize large-scale, expensive events like the Floriade. Preparation takes years and involves high costs and risks for the municipality and residents. Smaller, feasible events are preferred.

statements.title: Almere should stop organizing large events, such as the Floriade.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Almere should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

There should be a facility for people with addictions in Oosterwold.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Almere should introduce parking permits in more neighborhoods.

The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

The care of historic or special old buildings.

The BOAs who enforce safety in Almere should be given a baton.

statements.title: The new district Almere Pampus may only be developed if there is a car and metro connection to Amsterdam.
